perturbation.original_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account.
perturbation.changed_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account. However, COMPANY reserves the right to extend the payment term to NET 60 days at its sole discretion, based on COMPANY's cash flow situation.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in payment terms. The original text specifies 'NET 30 days' as the payment deadline. The added sentence allows COMPANY to unilaterally extend this to 'NET 60 days' based on their cash flow. This creates an in-text contradiction because Bravatek is unsure when they are going to be paid, and under what circumstances the extension occurs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account. However, COMPANY reserves the right to extend the payment term to NET 60 days at its sole discretion, based on COMPANY's cash flow situation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text mandates payment 'NET 30 days'. The added clause allows COMPANY to unilaterally extend this to 'NET 60 days' based on their cash flow, which directly contradicts the original payment term. This introduces uncertainty and conflict, making the original 'NET 30 days' term unenforceable as the COMPANY can change at will. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the notice period required for early termination. Originally, either party could terminate with 'ninety (90) days written notice.' This has been changed to 'thirty (30) days written notice'. This contradicts the original ninety (90) days, creating uncertainty about the actual requirements for termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination notice period from ninety (90) days to thirty (30) days. The contradicted text (which is incidentally the same as the changed text in this case, but that doesn't negate the contradiction created by the change itself) reflects this change, indicating a thirty (30) day notice period. The original text, specifying ninety (90) days for termination notice, directly conflicts with this altered period, creating an inconsistency regarding the correct notice period for early termination. This is a strong contradiction as it alters a fundamental term of the agreement regarding termination, thus impacting enforceability and leading to disputes as defined in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: COMPANY shall indemnify Bravatek, its directors, officers and employees, for any and all damages, costs, expenses, and other liabilities, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs incurred in connection with any third-party claim, action or proceeding arising from the negligence or intentional misconduct of COMPANY or breach of COMPANY of any of its obligations under this Agreement. Bravatek shall indemnify COMPANY, its directors, officers and employees, for any and all damages, costs, expenses, and other liabilities, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs, incurred in connection with any third-party claim, action or proceeding arising from the negligence or intentional misconduct of Bravatek or breach of Bravatek of any of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: COMPANY shall indemnify Bravatek, its directors, officers and employees, for any and all damages, costs, expenses, and other liabilities, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs incurred in connection with any third-party claim, action or proceeding arising from the gross negligence or intentional misconduct of COMPANY or breach of COMPANY of any of its obligations under this Agreement. Bravatek shall indemnify COMPANY, its directors, officers and employees, for any and all damages, costs, expenses, and other liabilities, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs, incurred in connection with any third-party claim, action or proceeding arising from the negligence or intentional misconduct of Bravatek or breach of Bravatek of any of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an inconsistency in the conditions for indemnification. Originally, COMPANY indemnified Bravatek for 'negligence or intentional misconduct.' The changed text modifies this to 'gross negligence or intentional misconduct.' However, Bravatek's indemnification of COMPANY remains based on 'negligence or intentional misconduct.' This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty. It suggests a higher standard for COMPANY's liability than for Bravatek's, which is contradictory and confusing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: COMPANY shall indemnify Bravatek, its directors, officers and employees, for any and all damages, costs, expenses, and other liabilities, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs incurred in connection with any third-party claim, action or proceeding arising from the gross negligence or intentional misconduct of COMPANY or breach of COMPANY of any of its obligations under this Agreement. Bravatek shall indemnify COMPANY, its directors, officers and employees, for any and all damages, costs, expenses, and other liabilities, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs, incurred in connection with any third-party claim, action or proceeding arising from the negligence or intentional misconduct of Bravatek or breach of Bravatek of any of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an inconsistency by requiring 'gross negligence' for COMPANY's indemnification obligation while maintaining 'negligence' for Bravatek's. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which reflects this change, creating an uneven standard of liability between the parties within the same clause. This represents a clear contradiction and directly aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as the two sections provide conflicting obligations.
